text.title: Rust Tokio Async Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of Rust Tokio Async. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Latency spikes; one worker thread frozen for 200ms on `std::fs::read`.
antipattern.root-cause: Sync fs call inside async handler.

antipattern.symptom: OOMKilled overnight; metrics show 10M messages queued.
antipattern.fix: Switch to bounded `mpsc::channel(1024)`; producer experiences back-pressure when full.

antipattern.symptom: spawn'd tasks panic silently; logs show nothing.
antipattern.fix: Use `JoinSet` and await results; panics propagate through `Result<...>` returned by `join_next`.

antipattern.symptom: Runtime hangs; all worker threads waiting on the same mutex.
antipattern.fix: Switch to `tokio::sync::Mutex` or restructure to drop the lock before await.
